raw_data.details: The “Annal Gandhi Memorial Award”, introduced on May 7, 2012, by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, aims to encourage academic excellence among students from the Hindu Adi Dravidar community. The scheme helps students pursue higher education through providing financial assistance.
raw_data.benefits: In first year: ₹2,000. For the succeeding five years: ₹1,500 per annum.
raw_data.eligibility: The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu. The beneficiary should be a Student. The beneficiary should belong to the SC/ST (Hindu Adi Dravidar) community. The beneficiary must have secured the first rank in plus 2 Public Examinations in their district.
raw_data.application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should collect the application form from the concerned educational institution.
Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the educational institution.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
raw_data.documents_required: Proof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority). Community certificate. Plus 2 Public Examination mark sheet. Bank account details. Any other documents (if required).

To know more please visit Check EligibilityAnnal Gandhi Memorial AwardTamil NaduAnnal Gandhi Memorial AwardAwardEducationExaminationTribal WelfareCheck EligibilityDetailsThe “Annal Gandhi Memorial Award”, introduced on May 7, 2012, by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, aims to encourage academic excellence among students from the Hindu Adi Dravidar community. The scheme helps students pursue higher education through providing financial assistance. BenefitsIn first year: ₹2,000. For the succeeding five years: ₹1,500 per annum. EligibilityThe beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu. The beneficiary should be a Student. The beneficiary should belong to the SC/ST (Hindu Adi Dravidar) community. The beneficiary must have secured the first rank in plus 2 Public Examinations in their district. Application ProcessOfflineStep 1: The interested applicant should collect the application form from the concerned educational institution. Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the educational institution. Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable). Documents RequiredProof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority). Community certificate. Plus 2 Public Examination mark sheet. Bank account details. Any other documents (if required). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the Annal Gandhi Memorial Award? The Annal Gandhi Memorial Award is a financial assistance program for SC/ST students, specifically from the Hindu Adi Dravidar community, who secure the first rank in plus 2 Public Examinations.

Who is eligible for the award? The award is given to one boy and one girl from the Hindu Adi Dravidar community who secure first rank in their district in the plus 2 Public Examinations.

How much financial assistance is provided? The selected students receive ₹2,000 in the first year and ₹1,500 per annum for the next five years.

How long is the award valid for? The award is valid for a period of six years - one year for the initial ₹2,000 and five years for the ₹1,500 per year.

Is there any income limit to apply for this award? No, there is no income limit mentioned for eligibility under this scheme.

Can students from other communities apply for this award? No, this award is exclusively for students from the Hindu Adi Dravidar community (SC/ST).

Is the award applicable for all students across the state? Yes, it is applicable to one boy and one girl from each district who meets the eligibility criteria.

What are the documents required to apply for this award? Students need to provide their Plus 2 Public Examination mark sheet and proof of community.

What is the process to apply for the award? Students can apply through their respective educational institutions by submitting the necessary documents.

When was the "Annal Gandhi Memorial Award" introduced? The award was introduced on May 7, 2012.

Where can students get more information about the award? Students can inquire with their educational institutions or local authorities for more details regarding the application process and eligibility.

Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.tn.gov.in/scheme/data_view/26775)OkWas this helpful?
